title: Phase F: Firebase auth upgrade — accounts, sign-in gate, user profiles
description:
Upgrade authentication so Firebase mode requires a real account (not anonymous).

src/services/storage/FirebaseAuth.ts:
- Add ensureUserProfile(user): writes/updates users/{userId} doc in Firestore on every sign-in
- Remove anonymous-as-default behaviour: in Firebase mode, do not auto-create anonymous users
- signInMagicLink remains the primary auth method

src/App.tsx:
- In Firebase mode: if no authenticated user and no magic link in progress, show a sign-in gate screen (email input → sends magic link)
- In local mode: keep existing anonymous auto-sign-in

Note: LocalAuth.ts anonymous mode remains unchanged for local development.

title: Phase E: UI — ProjectDashboard, SongMenu, MembersPanel, InviteAccept
description:
Build the 4 new UI components and update 3 existing ones.

NEW: src/components/ProjectDashboard.tsx
- Shown in App.tsx when currentSongId === null (no song loaded)
- Lists user's projects via storageService.listUserProjects()
- Shows pending invites for current user's email
- Click project → expand to show songs list (storageService.listSongs)
- Click song → calls store.loadSong + store.setCurrentProject → returns to DAW view
- 'New Project' button → creates project and enters it

NEW: src/components/SongMenu.tsx (replaces ProjectMenu.tsx)
- Lists songs within currentProjectId only
- Save/save-as creates songs under current project
- 'Back to Projects' button sets currentSongId to null → shows Dashboard

NEW: src/components/MembersPanel.tsx
- Tab or panel within CollaborationPanel
- List of members with name, role badge
- Invite form: email input + role dropdown + Send Invite (owner only)
- Pending invites list with Revoke button (owner only)
- Non-owners see member list read-only

NEW: src/components/InviteAccept.tsx
- Modal shown when ?invite=&project= detected in URL (set by Phase D)
- Shows project name, inviter, role
- If not signed in: show magic link sign-in form first
- 'Join Project' button → storageService.acceptInvite → dismiss, navigate to Dashboard

MODIFIED: src/App.tsx
- Conditional render: ProjectDashboard vs DAW canvas based on currentSongId
- Render InviteAccept modal when inviteParams state is set

MODIFIED: src/components/Toolbar.tsx
- FolderOpen button opens SongMenu (not ProjectMenu)
- Add breadcrumb in left section: [Project Name] / [Song Name]
- Clicking project name portion navigates back to Dashboard

MODIFIED: src/components/CollaborationPanel.tsx
- Add Members tab alongside existing comments view
- Update 'Project Hub' label to reflect new terminology

title: Phase D: Wire Zustand store & App to two-level hierarchy
description:
Update store internals and App.tsx so all sync/push/presence operations use the new projectId+songId two-level path.

src/store.ts:
- syncSong(projectId, songId): calls storageService.onSongUpdate and onPresenceUpdate with both IDs
- pushUpdate(): reads currentProjectId + currentSongId, calls storageService.saveSong(projectId, songId, data)
- updatePresence(cursorPosition): calls storageService.updatePresence(projectId, songId, cursorPosition)

src/App.tsx:
- Update sync useEffect: watch both currentProjectId and currentSongId
- Add invite param detection: after completeMagicLinkSignIn resolves, check ?invite=&project= in URL → set inviteParams state to trigger InviteAccept modal

src/hooks/usePresenceSync.ts:
- Read both currentProjectId and currentSongId from store, pass both to updatePresence

title: Phase C: Implement new Firestore & LocalStorage schema
description:
Implement all new StorageService methods for both Firebase and local backends.

FirebaseStorage.ts (src/services/storage/FirebaseStorage.ts):
- createProject(name): writes projects/{id}, members/{userId} (owner), userProjects/{userId}/projects/{id} mirror
- listUserProjects(): reads userProjects/{userId}/projects subcollection
- getSong/saveSong/listSongs/deleteSong: operate on projects/{projectId}/songs/{songId}
- getMembers(projectId): reads projects/{projectId}/members subcollection
- inviteToProject(projectId, email, role): writes invite doc + calls sendSignInLinkToEmail with ?invite=&project= params in URL
- acceptInvite(inviteId, projectId): validates email+expiry, writes member doc + userProjects mirror, marks invite accepted
- revokeInvite, listInvites
- updatePresence/onPresenceUpdate: updated signatures include songId

LocalStorage.ts (src/services/storage/LocalStorage.ts):
- IDB version bump to 2: migration renames 'projects' store → 'songs', adds new 'projects' store
- Implement song ops (getSong, saveSong, listSongs, deleteSong) against 'songs' store
- Implement project ops (createProject, listUserProjects) against 'projects' store
- Stub invite methods: throw 'Not supported in local mode'

firestore.rules: rewrite for new schema with member-based access guards.

Note on Firestore rules: existing rules use request.time.toMillis() on updatedAt; relax to ±5s tolerance to work with client-side Date.now().

title: Phase A: Unify storage service layer
description:
Two storage systems exist in parallel and must be consolidated before any new features are added.

What needs to be done:
- src/services/storage/index.ts: restore VITE_STORAGE_MODE env-based switching (currently hardcoded to local)
- src/components/ProjectMenu.tsx: remove direct setDoc(doc(db, 'projects', ...)) Firestore call; replace with storageService from storage/index.ts
- src/components/Toolbar.tsx: switch import from old src/services/storageService.ts to src/services/storage/index.ts
- Delete src/services/storageService.ts (legacy, superseded by storage/ layer)

Acceptance: app works identically in local mode after changes; no direct Firestore imports outside the storage/ service layer.

title: Phase B: Rename types — ProjectData→SongData, add Project/Member/Invite
description:
Align TypeScript types with the new terminology before implementing storage.

Changes to src/services/storage/types.ts:
- Rename ProjectData → SongData, add projectId field
- Add: Role = 'owner'|'editor'|'viewer', Member, Invite, Project interfaces
- Add songId to Presence
- Extend StorageService interface with all new method signatures (getSong, saveSong, createProject, listUserProjects, getMembers, inviteToProject, acceptInvite, etc.)

Changes to src/types.ts + src/store.ts:
- Rename currentProjectId/currentProjectName → currentSongId/currentSongName
- ADD currentProjectId, currentProjectName, currentUserRole: Role|null to DAWState
- Rename loadProject→loadSong, syncProject→syncSong actions

Acceptance: npm run build compiles with zero TypeScript errors.

title: Collaboration model: Projects, Songs & member invites
description:
Overhaul JackDAW's data model and UI to support a proper multi-user collaboration structure.

Currently the app has a flat 'project' concept (single DAW session) with no top-level grouping, no invite system, and broken terminology. This epic introduces:
- Project: top-level container (e.g. 'Doomsword Album') with members and invites
- Song: individual DAW session (renamed from 'project')
- Track: unchanged (individual audio stem)

Covers 6 phases: storage unification, type renames, Firebase/local implementation, store wiring, UI components, auth upgrade.

title: can't hear anything
notes: Root cause: onClick={importFiles} in Toolbar passed the MouseEvent as the offset argument. clip.offset became a SyntheticBaseEvent, all time math produced NaN, startTime < clipEnd was always false, so no audio sources were ever scheduled. Fix: onClick={() => importFiles()}
close_reason: AudioContext.resume() was not awaited; sources were scheduled while context was still suspended. Made startPlayback async and await the resume call.
